evals.prompt: Add a new team member named Bob who is a backend developer with expertise in Python and Go, and weakness in frontend. Bob should not be a leader. Then list all team members to verify.
evals.expected_output: Bob appears in the team list with correct role and expertise

evals.prompt: Add two team members to different groups: Alice in 'backend-team' group and Bob in 'frontend-team' group. Alice should be the leader. Then list the team to verify grouping works.
evals.expected_output: Members are listed under their respective groups in the output

evals.prompt: Add a team member with load_workflow set to false. Then add another member as leader with load_workflow set to true. List the team and verify load_workflow field is displayed correctly.
evals.expected_output: load_workflow values are correctly shown in list output

evals.prompt: Change the team leader from current leader to a different member. Verify the old leader loses leader status and new leader gains it. Only one leader should exist after the change.
evals.expected_output: Only one leader exists after the change

evals.prompt: Add a member with group and load_workflow fields set. Then update only their name and role (keeping other fields unchanged). Verify new fields are updated but existing optional fields are preserved.
evals.expected_output: Existing optional fields (group, load_workflow) are preserved after partial update
